paragraphs: Energy usage rose in winter because heating demand spiked across dense apartment blocks. Detached homes had flatter usage curves, but peak evening demand still increased.
The city rolled out a rebate program for efficient heat pumps. Adoption was uneven by district, and early savings were strongest in zones with older insulation.
By year-end, monthly totals stabilized. Peak-hour smoothing improved reliability metrics, while customer complaints about outages declined.
